You have been working on a feature locally on the `feature-login` bookmark. Meanwhile, your coworker pushed some new commits to the same `feature-login` bookmark on the `origin` remote.\n\nYour task is to:\n1. Fetch the latest changes from the `origin` remote.\n2. Observe that this creates a bookmark conflict for `feature-login` because it diverged.\n3. Resolve the bookmark conflict by rebasing your local commit(s) on top of the remote'\"'\"'s `feature-login` commit.\n4. Update the `feature-login` bookmark to point to your newly rebased commit.\n5. Push the resolved `feature-login` bookmark back to the `origin` remote.\n\nThe repository is located at `/home/user/repo`. The remote is an existing local bare repository at `/home/user/remote.git`.\n\nVerify your success by ensuring `jj git push` succeeds and the remote'\"'\"'s `feature-login` bookmark includes both your coworker'\"'\"'s changes and your local changes.\n'"}]}

I'm now exploring the help documentation to understand its behavior fully.\n\n\n","state":"done"},{"type":"tool-executeCommand","toolCallId":"teyrjLD0NyXHcgUH","state":"output-available","input":{"command":"cd /home/user/repo && jj git push --help"},"output":{"output":"Push to a Git remote\r\n\r\nBy default, pushes tracking bookmarks pointing to `remote_bookmarks(remote=<remote>)..@`. Use\r\n`--bookmark` to push specific bookmarks. Use `--all` to push all bookmarks. Use `--change` to\r\ngenerate bookmark names based on the change IDs of specific commits.\r\n\r\nWhen pushing a bookmark, the command pushes all commits in the range from the remote's current\r\nposition up to and including the bookmark's target commit. Any descendant commits beyond the\r\nbookmark are not pushed.\r\n\r\nIf the local bookmark has changed from the last fetch, push will update the remote bookmark to the\r\nnew position after passing safety checks. This is similar to `git push --force-with-lease` - the\r\nremote is updated only if its current state matches what Jujutsu last fetched.\r\n\r\nUnlike in Git, the remote to push to is not derived from the tracked remote bookmarks. Use\r\n`--remote` to select the remote Git repository by name. There is no option to push to multiple\r\nremotes.\r\n\r\nBefore the command actually moves, creates, or deletes a remote bookmark, it makes several [safety\r\nchecks]. If there is a problem, you may need to run `jj git fetch --remote <remote name>` and/or\r\nresolve some [bookmark conflicts].\r\n\r\n[safety checks]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/bookmarks/#pushing-bookmarks-safety-checks\r\n\r\n[bookmark conflicts]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/bookmarks/#conflicts\r\n\r\nUsage: jj git push [OPTIONS]\r\n\r\nOptions:\r\n      --remote <REMOTE>\r\n          The remote to push to (only named remotes are supported)\r\n          \r\n          This defaults to the `git.push` setting. If that is not configured, and if there are\r\n          multiple remotes, the remote named \"origin\" will be used.\r\n\r\n  -b, --bookmark <BOOKMARK>\r\n          Push only this bookmark, or bookmarks matching a pattern (can be repeated)\r\n          \r\n          If a bookmark isn't tracking anything yet, the remote bookmark will be tracked\r\n          automatically.\r\n          \r\n          By default, the specified pattern matches bookmark names with glob syntax. You can also\r\n          use other [string pattern syntax].\r\n          \r\n          [string pattern syntax]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/revsets/#string-patterns\r\n\r\n      --all\r\n          Push all bookmarks (including new bookmarks)\r\n\r\n      --tracked\r\n          Push all tracked bookmarks\r\n          \r\n          This usually means that the bookmark was already pushed to or fetched from the [relevant\r\n          remote].\r\n          \r\n          [relevant remote]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/bookmarks#remotes-and-tracked-bookmarks\r\n\r\n      --deleted\r\n          Push all deleted bookmarks\r\n          \r\n          Only tracked bookmarks can be successfully deleted on the remote. A warning will be\r\n          printed if any untracked bookmarks on the remote correspond to missing local bookmarks.\r\n\r\n      --allow-empty-description\r\n          Allow pushing commits with empty descriptions\r\n\r\n      --allow-private\r\n          Allow pushing commits that are private\r\n          \r\n          The set of private commits can be configured by the `git.private-commits` setting. The\r\n          default is `none()`, meaning all commits are eligible to be pushed.\r\n\r\n  -r, --revisions <REVSETS>\r\n          Push bookmarks pointing to these commits (can be repeated)\r\n\r\n  -c, --change <REVSETS>\r\n          Push this commit by creating a bookmark (can be repeated)\r\n          \r\n          The created bookmark will be tracked automatically. Use the `templates.git_push_bookmark`\r\n          setting to customize the generated bookmark name. The default is `\"push-\" ++\r\n          change_id.short()`.\r\n\r\n      --named <NAME=REVISION>\r\n          Specify a new bookmark name and a revision to push under that name, e.g. '--named\r\n          myfeature=@'\r\n          \r\n          Automatically tracks the bookmark if it is new.\r\n\r\n      --dry-run\r\n          Only display what will change on the remote\r\n\r\n  -h, --help\r\n          Print help (see a summary with '-h')\r\n\r\nGlobal Options:\r\n  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>\r\n          Path to repository to operate on\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu searches for the closest .jj/ directory in an ancestor of the current\r\n          working directory.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-working-copy\r\n          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu snapshots the working copy at the beginning of every command. The\r\n          working copy is also updated at the end of the command, if the command modified the\r\n          working-copy commit (`@`). If you want to avoid snapshotting the working copy and instead\r\n          see a possibly stale working-copy commit, you can use `--ignore-working-copy`. This may be\r\n          useful e.g. in a command prompt, especially if you have another process that commits the\r\n          working copy.\r\n          \r\n          Loading the repository at a specific operation with `--at-operation` implies\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy`.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-immutable\r\n          Allow rewriting immutable commits\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu prevents rewriting commits in the configured set of immutable commits.\r\n          This option disables that check and lets you rewrite any commit but the root commit.\r\n          \r\n          This option only affects the check. It does not affect the `immutable_heads()` revset or\r\n          the `immutable` template keyword.\r\n\r\n      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>\r\n          Operation to load the repo at\r\n          \r\n          Operation to load the repo at. By default, Jujutsu loads the repo at the most recent\r\n          operation, or at the merge of the divergent operations if any.\r\n          \r\n          You can use `--at-op=<operation ID>` to see what the repo looked like at an earlier\r\n          operation. For example `jj --at-op=<operation ID> st` will show you what `jj st` would\r\n          have shown you when the given operation had just finished. `--at-op=@` is pretty much the\r\n          same as the default except that divergent operations will never be merged.\r\n          \r\n          Use `jj op log` to find the operation ID you want. Any unambiguous prefix of the operation\r\n          ID is enough.\r\n          \r\n          When loading the repo at an earlier operation, the working copy will be ignored, as if\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy` had been specified.\r\n          \r\n          It is possible to run mutating commands when loading the repo at an earlier operation.\r\n          Doing that is equivalent to having run concurrent commands starting at the earlier\r\n          operation. There's rarely a reason to do that, but it is possible.\r\n          \r\n          [aliases: --at-op]\r\n\r\n      --debug\r\n          Enable debug logging\r\n\r\n      --color <WHEN>\r\n          When to colorize output\r\n          \r\n          [possible values: always, never, debug, auto]\r\n\r\n      --quiet\r\n          Silence non-primary command output\r\n          \r\n          For example, `jj file list` will still list files, but it won't tell you if the working\r\n          copy was snapshotted or if descendants were rebased.\r\n          \r\n          Warnings and errors will still be printed.\r\n\r\n      --no-pager\r\n          Disable the pager\r\n\r\n      --config <NAME=VALUE>\r\n          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)\r\n          \r\n          The name should be specified as TOML dotted keys. The value should be specified as a TOML\r\n          expression.
